model_input:
You are a UI clicking agent. Given a screenshot of a screen, determine the pixel coordinates of the target element.

Call click_at(x, y, confidence) with the center pixel coordinates of the target element.

Call click_at().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants me to click on the element with the text "CLICK ME".
Looking at the image, there is a green button in the center that says "CLICK ME".
I need to find the center coordinates of this button.

The button is located roughly at:
x: from 452 to 548
y: from 461 to 543

Center x = (452 + 548) / 2 = 1000 / 2 = 500
Center y = (461 + 543) / 2 = 1004 / 2 = 502

Let me re-verify the coordinates.
The green area is bounded by a white border.
White border left: 451
White border right: 549
White border top: 459
White border bottom: 545

Center x = (451 + 549) / 2 = 500
Center y = (459 + 545) / 2 = 502

Confidence is high as there is only one element.
